title: boundary-policy
description: Schema for x-harness boundary policy file (policies/boundaries.yaml). V1 supports path-glob + import-regex enforcement only; AST parsers, semgrep, and codeql backends are explicitly out of scope for V1. Rules are applied during `xh boundary check` and surfaced as advisory or runtime_blocking depending on the active verify profile (V1 default: advisory only; admission integration per profile is opt-in).

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the policy file.

boundaries.description: Boundary rules. Each rule is a deterministic path-glob + import-regex predicate. Rules are evaluated in declared order; a file is checked against every rule whose `from` glob matches the file path.

id.description: Stable rule identifier. Used as `rule_id` in violation output and as a discriminator in CI tooling.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in `xh boundary explain`.

from.description: Forward-slash glob (with `**` and `*`) matched against the candidate file's repo-relative path. A leading `./` is stripped before matching.

to_import.description: Substring or simple glob matched against an import path extracted from a single line. `**` is treated like `*`. An exact import line is a violation when this pattern matches the extracted import path.

action.description: Action when the rule matches. `deny` raises a violation; `require_intermediate` raises a violation unless the import path also matches `intermediate`; `warn` raises a warning-level finding.

severity.description: Severity classification. The default V1 policy uses `warning` for advisory rules. Per-profile severity escalation is a V2 concern.

intermediate.description: Required intermediate path or import glob for `require_intermediate` rules. The violation is suppressed when the import path also contains/matches this substring/glob.

allow.description: Path/import glob exceptions. A violation is suppressed when the import path or file path matches any entry.

applies_to_languages.description: Restrict the rule to specific languages (by file extension). When omitted, the rule applies to all supported languages.
